# UC-23.9.3 · Waste Diversion and Recycling Rate Tracking

## Description

Tracks waste generation by type (landfill, recycling, composting, hazardous) and calculates diversion rates against zero-waste targets. Operations and sustainability teams see which facilities and waste streams need attention, while the data feeds directly into ESG disclosure requirements — many organisations now report waste metrics to investors and rating agencies.

## Value

Tracks waste generation by type (landfill, recycling, composting, hazardous) and calculates diversion rates against zero-waste targets. Operations and sustainability teams see which facilities and waste streams need attention, while the data feeds directly into ESG disclosure requirements — many organisations now report waste metrics to investors and rating agencies.

## Implementation

(1) Import waste manifests from waste management provider via CSV upload or HEC; (2) classify disposal methods consistently; (3) schedule monthly; (4) set diversion rate targets by facility; (5) alert when any site's diversion rate drops below target; (6) trend quarterly for ESG reporting.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686), HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=business` `sourcetype="waste_manifest"` (site, waste_type, weight_kg, disposal_method, collection_date).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Import waste manifests from waste management provider via CSV upload or HEC; (2) classify disposal methods consistently; (3) schedule monthly; (4) set diversion rate targets by facility; (5) alert when any site's diversion rate drops below target; (6) trend quarterly for ESG reporting.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=business sourcetype="waste_manifest" earliest=-90d
| eval diverted=if(disposal_method IN ("recycled","composted","reused"), 1, 0)
| stats sum(weight_kg) as total_kg, sum(eval(if(diverted=1,weight_kg,0))) as diverted_kg by site
| eval diversion_rate=round(100*diverted_kg/total_kg, 1)
| eval landfill_kg=total_kg-diverted_kg
| sort - landfill_kg
| table site, total_kg, diverted_kg, landfill_kg, diversion_rate
```

Understanding this SPL

**Waste Diversion and Recycling Rate Tracking** — Tracks waste generation by type (landfill, recycling, composting, hazardous) and calculates diversion rates against zero-waste targets. Operations and sustainability teams see which facilities and waste streams need attention, while the data feeds directly into ESG disclosure requirements — many organisations now report waste metrics to investors and rating agencies.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=business` `sourcetype="waste_manifest"` (site, waste_type, weight_kg, disposal_method, collection_date). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686), HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: business; **sourcetype**: waste_manifest. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=business, sourcetype="waste_manifest", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **diverted** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by site** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for comparisons across those breakdowns for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **diversion_rate** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **landfill_kg** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **Waste Diversion and Recycling Rate Tracking**): table site, total_kg, diverted_kg, landfill_kg, diversion_rate


Step 3 — Validate
Compare diversion and recycling rates to waste-vendor reports and the ESG workbook for the same sites, waste streams, and reporting year.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (diversion rate by site), Pie chart (waste by type), Line chart (monthly diversion trend), Single value (overall diversion rate %).

## SPL

```spl
index=business sourcetype="waste_manifest" earliest=-90d
| eval diverted=if(disposal_method IN ("recycled","composted","reused"), 1, 0)
| stats sum(weight_kg) as total_kg, sum(eval(if(diverted=1,weight_kg,0))) as diverted_kg by site
| eval diversion_rate=round(100*diverted_kg/total_kg, 1)
| eval landfill_kg=total_kg-diverted_kg
| sort - landfill_kg
| table site, total_kg, diverted_kg, landfill_kg, diversion_rate
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (diversion rate by site), Pie chart (waste by type), Line chart (monthly diversion trend), Single value (overall diversion rate %).
